Question 2 (3 marks)
Consider the simultaneous equations
3mx + 2ny = 5 + 3n
(m + n)x + (3n – 2m)y = 5m + 3n
where x, y ∈ R and m and n are real constants. 
Find the values of m and n for which the system of equations has infinite solutions.

Section B 
Instructions 
•	
Answer all questions in the spaces provided. 
•	
Write your responses in English. 
•	
In all questions where a numerical answer is required, an exact value must be given unless 
otherwise specified. 
•	
In questions where more than one mark is available, appropriate working must be shown. 
•	
Unless otherwise indicated, the diagrams in this booklet are not drawn to scale.

Question 6 (10 marks)
A person on a nature hike is walking along a horizontal path. They start from point A and walk 
past points B, C and D, and then to the end of the path at point E. The route from point A to point B 
is underneath a mountain, the route from point B to point C is across a bridge that is above a valley, 
and the route from point C to point D is underneath another mountain.
The distance from point D to point E is π
3 km.

where a, b and c are integers. The following graph represents this relationship and the route 
from point D to point E.
